quiz.question: Which parts of your face do you use to look at the bright colours in a picture book?

answerOptions.rationale: These are used for collecting sounds rather than processing visual information.
These organs are specifically designed to detect light and allow us to see the world around us.
This part is primarily used for eating and speaking, not for vision.
The function of this part is to breathe and detect different scents.

quiz.hint: Think about which part of your head helps you see things.

quiz.question: If a bell rings at school, which part of your head helps you to hear the sound?

answerOptions.rationale: Visual organs help us see the bell but do not process the sound it makes.
We have two of these on the sides of our head specifically for listening to sounds.
These are hard structures used for chewing food, not for hearing.
These are found on the hands and are used for touching or gripping objects.

quiz.hint: You have one of these on each side of your head to listen to music or voices.

quiz.question: Which part of your face would you use to find out if a flower has a lovely scent?

answerOptions.rationale: While the mouth is on the face, it is used for tasting and talking rather than smelling.
This is a joint in the leg used for bending, not a facial feature.
This part of the face contains the sensory receptors needed to detect smells.
Hands are used for touching and holding, but they cannot detect scents.

quiz.hint: This part is located in the middle of your face.

quiz.question: Which part of your head is used for both speaking to your friends and eating your lunch?

answerOptions.rationale: The head is the whole top section of the body, but a specific part within it performs these actions.
This opening allows us to take in food and use our tongue and lips to form words.
The foot is at the bottom of the body and is used for walking.
Hands may hold the food, but they are not the part used to chew or speak.

quiz.hint: It is the part of your face that moves when you talk or chew.

quiz.question: What is the name of the long part of your upper body that connects your shoulder to your hand?

answerOptions.rationale: The leg is part of the lower body and connects the hip to the foot.
The foot is the very end of the leg, not the connector between the shoulder and hand.
This limb serves as the main link between the torso and the hand.
The head is at the top of the body and does not connect the shoulder to the hand.

quiz.hint: This part includes your biceps and your elbow.

quiz.question: Which long body parts allow you to run quickly around the playground?

answerOptions.rationale: Arms may swing while running, but they do not provide the power to move across the ground.
Ears are for hearing and do not play a role in physical movement across a distance.
These lower limbs are the primary parts used for walking, running, and jumping.
Hands are used for holding things, not for supporting the body while running.

quiz.hint: These start at your hips and end at your feet.

quiz.question: What are the small, thin parts on your hand that help you pick up a tiny marble?

answerOptions.rationale: Toes are found on the feet and are not usually used for fine gripping tasks.
We have five of these on each hand to help us grip, touch, and feel small objects.
The arm is the large limb, while the smaller parts at the end do the actual gripping.
Legs are used for movement and are much too large for picking up marbles.

quiz.hint: You have ten of these in total on your two hands.

quiz.question: Which parts of your body do you place inside your socks and shoes?

answerOptions.rationale: Hands are usually kept in gloves or mittens rather than socks and shoes.
Arms are covered by sleeves, not by footwear.
These parts are at the bottom of our legs and need protection from the ground.
The head is covered by a hat, not by shoes.

quiz.hint: These are at the very bottom of your legs.

quiz.question: When you want to drink a glass of water, which part of your arm do you bend to move the glass to your mouth?

answerOptions.rationale: This joint in the middle of the arm allows it to fold so the hand can reach the face.
The knee is in the leg and does not help move the arm toward the mouth.
The foot is too far away from the glass and the mouth to assist in drinking.
Fingers hold the glass, but the arm itself bends at a different point to lift it.

quiz.hint: It is the hinge-like joint in the middle of your arm.

quiz.question: Which part of your leg must bend so that you can sit down on a small chair?

answerOptions.rationale: The elbow is part of the arm and is not used to sit down.
This joint allows the leg to fold, which is necessary for sitting or kneeling.
The hand is used for holding, not for bending the leg.
The nose is a facial feature and has no role in the movement of the legs.

quiz.hint: It is the hard, round part in the middle of your leg.

quiz.question: What is the specific name for the hard bone in your head that keeps your brain safe?

answerOptions.rationale: This refers to the bones in your hand, which do not protect the brain.
The skull is the part of the head skeleton designed to encase and protect the brain.
The foot bones are located at the bottom of the body and serve a different purpose.
The arm bones are for movement and reaching, not for protecting the head.

quiz.hint: It is also known as the head skeleton.

quiz.question: What do we call the whole collection of hard, white bones that are inside our body?

answerOptions.rationale: A school is a place where we learn, not a part of our internal body structure.
The skeleton is the framework of bones that supports our body and protects our organs.
A jacket is a piece of clothing worn on the outside of the body.
A classroom is a room within a building, not an internal body part.

quiz.hint: This internal frame helps you stand up straight.

quiz.question: What can you feel if you press on the hard parts inside your fingers or your shins?

answerOptions.rationale: While our bodies contain water, it is liquid and not the hard structure we feel under the skin.
Food is processed in the stomach and is not part of the structure of the fingers or shins.
Bones are the hard pieces of the skeleton that provide structure and can be felt through the skin.
Hair grows on the surface of the skin and is soft, not hard and internal.

quiz.hint: These make up the skeleton inside your body.

quiz.question: Why is it important to have bones inside our arms and legs?

answerOptions.text: To help us stand and move
answerOptions.rationale: Bones provide the rigid structure needed for muscles to pull against so we can move and stay upright.
Sleep is a biological process that does not directly depend on the presence of bones in the limbs.
Eye colour is determined by genetics and the iris, not by the bones in our arms or legs.
While the jawbone helps with chewing, the bones in our limbs are for posture and locomotion.

quiz.hint: Think about how you would stay upright if your body was just soft skin and muscle.

quiz.question: What are the small parts at the very end of your feet called?

answerOptions.rationale: Fingers are the equivalent parts found on the hands, not the feet.
These are the ten small digits found at the front of the feet.
Knees are the joints in the middle of the leg, far from the end of the foot.
Eyes are facial features used for seeing and are not located on the feet.

quiz.hint: You have five of these on each foot.

quiz.question: Which part of your skeleton is found at the very bottom and helps you balance when you stand up?

answerOptions.rationale: The head skeleton is at the top and protects the brain, but it doesn't touch the ground for balance.
The hands are for grasping and are not typically used to balance the body while standing.
The bones in the feet provide a wide base that supports the weight of the entire body.
Arm bones are designed for reaching and lifting rather than supporting standing weight.

quiz.hint: It is the set of bones inside the part of your body that touches the floor.

quiz.question: When you nod your face up and down to say 'yes' without using words, which part are you moving?

answerOptions.rationale: Moving the foot is not a standard gesture for agreeing with someone.
Nodding involves moving the entire top section of the body where the face is located.
Moving the hand might be a wave, but it is not a 'nod'.
Toes are too small and too far down to be seen clearly during a conversation.

quiz.hint: This part is at the very top of your body.

quiz.question: Which two body parts do you bring together to make a loud sound when you are clapping for a friend?

answerOptions.rationale: Stamping feet makes a sound, but 'clapping' specifically refers to the hands.
Clapping is the action of striking the palms of the hands together.
Legs are used for walking and running, not for making a clapping sound.
The head is not used as a percussion instrument during clapping.

quiz.hint: They are at the ends of your arms.

quiz.question: Which part of the leg makes contact with the ball when you want to kick it across the field?

answerOptions.rationale: Using hands to touch the ball is usually not allowed in games like football.
The foot is the part of the leg used to strike a ball for kicking.
The arm is in the upper body and is not the primary part used for kicking.
While you can 'head' a ball, the question asks for the part of the leg used.

quiz.hint: This is the same part you put your shoes on.

quiz.question: On which part of your body does your hair usually grow?

answerOptions.rationale: While some hair can grow on legs, the most visible and thickest hair grows elsewhere.
Hair on the arms is usually very fine compared to the hair mentioned in the source.
The scalp on the head is where our main head of hair grows.
Hair does not typically grow in a significant way on the toes.

quiz.hint: This part is at the very top of your body.

quiz.question: Which parts of your hand are essential for gripping a spoon or a pencil?

answerOptions.rationale: Toes are on the feet and are not used for writing or eating with a spoon.
The digits on the hand allow for the precision and grip needed to hold small tools.
Legs support our weight but do not have the ability to grip a pencil.
The head does not have any parts that can grip a pencil for writing.

quiz.hint: You use these small digits to hold onto things tightly.

quiz.question: What do we call the very bottom part of the leg that touches the floor when you walk?

answerOptions.rationale: The arm is part of the upper body and does not touch the floor when walking.
Hands are used for touching things, but not for supporting the body during walking.
The foot is the base of the leg and the point of contact with the ground.
The mouth is for eating and speaking and is nowhere near the floor during walking.

quiz.hint: You have two of these, and they fit into your shoes.

quiz.question: Which joint allows you to fold your leg so you can crouch down low to the ground?

answerOptions.rationale: The nose is on the face and does not bend to help the body crouch.
Hair is a thread-like growth and not a joint used for movement.
The knee is the hinge joint in the leg that allows for crouching and sitting.
Teeth are hard structures for chewing and do not assist in leg movement.

quiz.hint: It is located in the middle of your leg.

quiz.question: What do the hand and fingers work together to do when you are using a pencil to write your name?

answerOptions.rationale: Running is an activity performed by the legs and feet.
The fingers provide the grip and the hand provides the stability to guide the pencil.
Smelling is the job of the nose, not the hands or fingers.
Listening is the function of the ears.

quiz.hint: Think about how you grip a pen or pencil.

quiz.question: What is the joint that connects your arm to the rest of your torso at the top?

answerOptions.rationale: The arm starts at this point on the upper body and extends down to the hand.
The knee is in the middle of the leg, not the top of the arm.
The foot is at the bottom of the body and does not connect limbs to the torso.
Toes are at the very end of the feet.

quiz.hint: Your arm starts at this part of your upper body.

quiz.question: How many toes does a person usually have in total on both feet?

answerOptions.rationale: This is the number of toes on just one foot, not both combined.
Since we have two feet and five toes on each, the total is ten.
This is the number of feet we have, not the number of toes.
This would be the total if we counted both fingers and toes together.

quiz.hint: It is the same as the total number of fingers on two hands.
